prompt:
A small rotary potentiometer lying on a wooden electronics workbench, with its knurled metal shaft pointing upward and three solder pins visible along the bottom edge. A simple black knob is pushed onto the shaft, showing a white indicator line that could be used to set the position. In the background, a partially wired breadboard and a few resistors and jumper wires are softly out of focus, suggesting that this variable resistor is used for adjusting signal levels in basic circuits.

Near-future solarpunk electronics aesthetic with warm, soft lighting, neatly arranged tools, and a hint of greenery or a tiny solar gadget at the edge of the frame. Photorealistic 512×512 rendering, no brand logos or legible text on the potentiometer or surrounding objects. The composition should clearly emphasize the potentiometer and its knob as a standalone inventory item.
